title: About Me
text:
I am a Statistics and Data Science student at UCLA interested in statistical modeling, data analysis, and quantitative research. My work focuses on using data to better understand real-world problems and communicate insights clearly through analysis and visualization.
Through coursework and projects, I have worked with methods including regression modeling, classification algorithms, ANOVA, and broader statistical learning techniques. I enjoy combining technical analysis with clear explanations so that complex findings can be communicated in a way that is both rigorous and accessible.
My interests span several areas including data science, applied statistics, quantitative finance, and research that uses data to study social and economic systems. I am particularly interested in how statistical methods can reveal patterns in large datasets and support evidence-based decision making.
Beyond coursework, I enjoy building projects that combine statistical methods with real datasets, whether through predictive modeling, exploratory data analysis, experimental design, or public-facing digital research.

title: About Me
text:
What I Value

title: Statistics, data science, and quantitative analysis for real-world problems.

I am interested in applying statistical and computational methods to understand complex datasets and support data-driven decision making. My work ranges from statistical learning and experimental design to digital humanities and applied analytics, with growing interests in quantitative finance, risk, and strategy.

title: Statistics, data science, and quantitative analysis for real-world problems.
text:
Welcome
I am a UCLA Statistics and Data Science student interested in statistical modeling, machine learning, data analysis, and research communication. I enjoy building work that combines technical rigor with clarity, whether that means developing predictive models, designing experiments, or presenting insights through polished reports and public-facing websites.
Across coursework, internships, and independent projects, I have worked on problems involving classification, inference, visualization, and applied analytics. I am especially interested in opportunities where strong analytical methods can support better decision making.

title: Selected Work
text:
Selected Work
A selection of projects that reflect how I approach data: rigorous methods, clear communication, and thoughtful analysis.



 Beyond the Board

A digital humanities project examining structural inequality in education through historical context, policy analysis, and narrative storytelling.


The project explores how historical policy decisions, socioeconomic conditions, and racial disparities shape educational opportunity over time.


 View Website



 Predicting US Skin Cancer Status

A statistical learning project building classification models to predict benign versus malignant skin cancer cases using more than 50,000 observations.


Compared multiple models including GLM, LDA, and Random Forest. LDA was selected as the final model based on predictive performance and interpretability.


 Research Paper



 Music, Exercise Intensity, and Performance

A factorial ANOVA experiment analyzing how music genre and exercise intensity influence running performance.


Results showed exercise intensity had the strongest effect on running time, while music condition produced smaller but measurable differences.


 Research Paper
